title: anagallis pumila var. djalonis
intro: Anagallis pumila var. djalonis, also known as Dwarf Blue Pimpernel, is a small, annual or biennial plant species native to West Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Anagallis pumila var. djalonis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant's leaves and stems are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
